TREES DOWN AND DAMAGE TO MOBILE HOMES NORTH OF PORTAL AROUND MOORE ROAD AND OLD PORTAL ROAD. LARGE HAIL OF UNKNOWN SIZE FELL. INJURIES UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME. EMERGENCY CREWS ARE AT THE SCENE.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN ON JOE STEPHENS ROAD. SOME AS BIG AS 30 INCHES IN DIAMETER.

STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED EF0 TORNADO WITH PATH LENGTH OF 0.6 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS. TWO MOBILE HOMES HAD SIDING AND UNDER-SIDING DAMAGED ALONG WITH SOME MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE. DOZENS OF TREES SNAPPED AND UPROOTED.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. 1 MOBILE HOME DESTROYED ON LONNIE BURKE RD. 2 MOBILE HOMES DAMAGED ON MOTES RD.

STORM SURVEY CONFIRMED EF1 TORNADO WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 3.1 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 0.25 MILE. ONE MOBILE HOME COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND THROWN 30 YARDS. SEVERAL OTHER STRUCTURES DAMAGED. HUNDREDS OF TREES SNAPPED OR UPROOTED.

FOUR STRUCTURES RECEIVED DAMAGE WITH MULTIPLE TREES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PECAN GROVE ROAD AND CL WOODS ROAD. ONE CHURCH HAD STEEPLE AND PORCH DAMAGE. ONE MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED OVER AND DESTROYED. ONE TREE FELL ON ANOTHER MOBILE HOME. ONE HOUSE HAD A FRONT PORCH COLLAPSED. EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AND NWS PERSONNEL DETERMINED ALL DAMAGE WAS DUE TO STRONG STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.

*** 6 INJ *** NWS DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM HAS CONFIRMED A TORNADO MOVED VERY NEAR SAINT STEPHEN SOUTH CAROLINA. THE TORNADO DESTROYED A CHURCH AND INJURED 6 PEOPLE. TEAM IS STILL EVALUATING THE SEVERITY AND EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE. INITIAL TOUCH DOWN MAY HAVE OCCURRED AS FAR WEST AS RUSSELLVILLE. MORE INFORMATION INCLUDING A FINAL EF RATING WILL BE PROVIDED LATER TONIGHT OR ON SUNDAY.
GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL REPORTED NEAR TREE FARM LANE AND HWY 311.

EF-1 TORNADO CONFIRMED. TORNADIC WATERSPOUT FORMED OVER N. LAKE MOULTRIE AT 503 PM AND TRAVELED E NE 2 MILES BEFORE COMING ONSHORE 2.5 MILES SW OF RUSSELLVILLE. TORNADO TRAVELED E NE BEFORE DISSIPATING 1 MILE SE OF ST. STEPHEN. THE TORNADO LIFTED A ROOF OFF A CHURCH...DESTROYED A MOBILE HOME...SHIFTED TWO MOBILE HOMES OFF FOUNDATIONS...TORE ROOF OFF A HOUSE...DESTROYED A BARN...TORE SIDING OFF OF 12 PLUS MOBILE HOMES...UPROOTED MANY HUNDRED TREES...DESTROYED FENCES AND DOWNED POWER POLES.

*** 6 INJ *** NWS DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM HAS CONFIRMED A TORNADO MOVED VERY NEAR SAINT STEPHEN SOUTH CAROLINA. THE TORNADO DESTROYED A CHURCH AND INJURED 6 PEOPLE. TEAM IS STILL EVALUATING THE SEVERITY AND EXTENT OF THE DAMAGE. INITIAL TOUCH DOWN MAY HAVE OCCURRED AS FAR WEST AS RUSSELLVILLE. MORE INFORMATION INCLUDING A FINAL EF RATING WILL BE PROVIDED LATER TONIGHT OR ON SUNDAY.
A FIRE DEPARTMENT OFFICIAL REPORTED A FULLY COLLAPSED CHURCH WITH 6 PEOPLE INJURED IN BETWEEN SAINT STEPHEN SOUTH CAROLINA AND BONNEAU SOUTH CAROLINA ALONG WITH LIVE POWER LINES DOWN ON THE GROUND.
